pr.title: mn: match mnitemsw, mnstagesw, and mnvibration functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: WIP, closed-unmerged attempt to match several menu settings functions in mnitemsw, mnstagesw, and mnvibration. The patch filled previously empty item/stage switch source and header files, added typed prototypes and globals, implemented list traversal helpers, menu transition/setup functions, language-dependent item table lookup, stage switch persistence, and several vibration helper functions. Review feedback focused on unfinished or questionable mnvibration work, especially wrapping HSD_GObj with ad-hoc offset structs and appearing to delete/rework an existing function without enough explanation.
what_changed: Added mnitemsw implementations for mnItemSw_80233A98, mnItemSw_8023405C, and mnItemSw_802358C0, plus ItemSwTable, globals mnItemSw_803ED340 and mnItemSw_804D6BEC, and public prototypes in mnitemsw.h.
Implemented mnItemSw_80233A98 as a table lookup with special cases for item IDs 0x11 and 0x13 when the language setting is not Japanese, returning 0x28 and 0x27 respectively.
Implemented mnItemSw_8023405C as a two-bucket linked-list traversal helper using an index split at 16, following x10 for the first child and x8 for siblings while preserving explicit NULL checks.

Implemented mnItemSw_802358C0 to update MenuFlow state to menu 0x10, reset hovered_selection, set mnItemSw_804D6BEC, call mnItemSw_802351A0, create a GObj, attach fn_80233E10 as a proc, and assign proc->flags_3 from HSD_GObj_804D783C.
Added mnstagesw implementations for mnStageSw_8023593C, mnStageSw_802364A0, and mnStageSw_80237410, plus globals mnStageSw_803ED4C4 and mnStageSw_804D6BF4 and public prototypes in mnstagesw.h.
Implemented mnStageSw_8023593C to iterate 0x1D stage entries from mnStageSw_803ED4C4, call gm_801641CC/gm_80164430/gm_801641E4, and write stage switch settings based on GObj user data at offset 0x2C.
Implemented mnStageSw_802364A0 as a two-bucket linked-list traversal helper using an index split at 15, analogous to the item switch helper but with node pointers at offsets 0x2C and 0x34.

Implemented mnStageSw_80237410 to update MenuFlow state to menu 0x11, initialize hovered_selection through mnStageSw_80235C58(0), set mnStageSw_804D6BF4, call HSD_GObj_80390CD4 on mnStageSw_80236CBC(1), create a proc for fn_80235F80, and set proc->flags_3.

Expanded mnvibration.h with VibNode, VibNodeContainer, VibData, VibJObjData, typed prototypes for mnVibration_802474C4, fn_80248084, and fn_80248748, and externs for mnVibration_803EECEC and mnVibration_804D6C28.

Added mnVibration_802474C4 linked-list traversal through mnVibration_804D6C28->x2C->x50, fn_80248084 to destroy/unlink a GObj when cur_menu is not 0x13, and fn_80248748 to unlink after an animation reaches end_frame.

Reworked mnVibration_80249174 formatting and setup to load vibration archive sections, call mnVibration_80248ED4 through a cast, create a GObj proc for fn_80247510, and set proc->flags_3 instead of manually bit-twiddling the GObj flag byte.
smart_moves: Using HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54's returned HSD_GObjProc and assigning proc->flags_3 = HSD_GObj_804D783C is cleaner and more structurally meaningful than manually editing the GObj flag byte at offset 0xD.

The item and stage switch traversal helpers preserve likely match-sensitive NULL-check structure while walking x10 child and x8 sibling links, which is a common decomp tactic for matching compiler output around linked UI node trees.
The PR moved several unknown functions from placeholder UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS toward typed signatures where enough evidence was available, improving callsite type checking and future matching work.
The mnitemsw language-specific table lookup captures JP/non-JP behavior directly with lbLang_IsSettingJP, making regional behavior explicit instead of hiding it behind magic table values.
decomp_lessons: When matching menu code that traverses JObj/GObj-associated UI trees, small offset structs can be useful locally, but reviewers may object if they wrap known engine types like HSD_GObj instead of using existing definitions.

If a PR is WIP or includes large replacement/deletion of existing functions, call that out prominently and explain the matching rationale; otherwise reviewers may interpret it as accidental code loss.
Prefer existing baselib structs and fields, such as HSD_GObjProc::flags_3, over raw byte-offset manipulation once the field is known.

For settings menus, MenuFlow updates are consistent: save prev_menu, assign cur_menu to the target menu ID, initialize hovered_selection, set a menu-specific active flag, create a GObj, attach the menu proc, and set proc flags.
Header work should expose only the structs and externs needed across translation units; local node-data structs can stay in .c files unless shared by multiple functions/files.
naming_conventions: Matched menu functions use module-prefixed names with address suffixes: mnItemSw_80233A98, mnStageSw_80237410, mnVibration_80249174.
Unidentified local/static functions retain fn_ address names such as fn_80233E10, fn_80235F80, fn_80248084, and fn_80248748.
Globals use module prefix plus address, such as mnItemSw_803ED340, mnStageSw_803ED4C4, mnVibration_804A0868, and mnVibration_804D6C28.
Temporary or reverse-engineered struct fields use offset names such as x2C, x34, x50, x8, and x10.
Header guards follow MELEE_MN_ITEMSW_H and MELEE_MN_STAGESW_H style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved redundant-looking NULL branches in linked-list traversal helpers, likely because simplifying them could alter generated control flow.
Used split-index traversal for item and stage switch UI node lists: item switch splits at 16, stage switch splits at 15.
Inserted dummy stack storage in mnStageSw_8023593C with s32 dummy[3]; (void) dummy;, suggesting a stack-layout or matching tactic, though the PR context does not explain it.
Used a function-pointer cast when calling mnVibration_80248ED4(arg0), indicating the target prototype was still unresolved or mismatched.
Replaced raw flag-byte manipulation with typed proc->flags_3 assignment to match known HSD_GObjProc layout.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Why are we making a wrapper type for a GObj instead of using it directly?
review_feedback.lesson: Avoid creating wrapper structs around known engine types when the actual HSD_GObj type can be used.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Very confused why an entire function was deleted?
review_feedback.lesson: Large removals or replacements in decomp PRs need explicit justification, especially in WIP slices.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.comment: I shoulda called out that this one is still a WIP - will clean this stuff up
review_feedback.lesson: Mark WIP status clearly and separate cleanup/matching experiments from ready-for-review changes.

follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs for mnitemsw.c, mnstagesw.c, or mnvibration.c to see which parts of PR #2077 were reused or corrected.
Search for reviewer guidance on using HSD_GObj directly versus ad-hoc wrapper structs in menu decomp code.
Check current mnvibration.h and mn/types.h for final accepted definitions of VibNode, VibData, VibJObjData, and AnimLoopSettings.
Compare accepted implementations of mnItemSw_8023405C and mnStageSw_802364A0 for linked-list traversal patterns and index split constants.
Investigate whether dummy stack arrays like dummy[3] in mnStageSw_8023593C are still used as matching tactics or were replaced by cleaner struct typing.
